message:
feat(ars): add AST symbolic execution for command safety verification

Implements lightweight shell AST parser + symbolic executor (ft-tkpgy.2.3)
that proves extracted commands don't escape CWD boundary. Shell lexer
handles quotes, pipes, redirects, subshells. Path resolver normalizes
relative/absolute paths. Detects: path traversal, banned binaries (mkfs,
dd, sudo), fork bombs, unbounded deletion, privilege escalation.
53 inline + 16 proptest = 69 tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.2.1.3","title":"[AARSP][Subtask][Verify] ft-2p9cb.2.1 :: Unit/property/integration/e2e + evidence gates","description":"## Objective\nProve correctness, performance, and resilience for the parent task with reproducible evidence.\n\n## Deliverables\n1.  {"id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2","title":"[AARSP][B2] Wait-free input ring and bounded queue primitives","description":"## Objective
Implement wait-free or bounded lock-free input ring with strict backpressure guarantees.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Input ring operations remain bounded under contention.
2. Throughput and latency benchmarks meet contract targets.
3. Unit/property tests validate no loss/corruption across edge cases.
4. E2E scripts validate sustained high-rate input behavior.

## User Impact
Reduced input jitter and lower dropped-interaction risk.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:05.644840Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:26.883530Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","performance","quality-gate","reactivity","reliability","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","wait-free"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1195,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Execution split added for implementation rigor and testability:\n1. ft-2p9cb.2.2.1 (spec/contracts/benchmark/test matrix)\n2. ft-2p9cb.2.2.2 (deterministic implementation + guardrails)\n3. ft-2p9cb.2.2.3 (unit/property/integration/e2e + evidence/CI gates)\n\nDependency chain: ft-2p9cb.2.2.2 -> ft-2p9cb.2.2.1 and ft-2p9cb.2.2.3 -> ft-2p9cb.2.2.2.\nParent-child linkage keeps the subtask relationship explicit while preserving DAG integrity.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:26Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.2.3","title":"[AARSP][B3] Priority inheritance and lock-order correctness","description":"## Objective
Add priority inheritance and lock-order protocol to eliminate inversion on critical interactions.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Priority inversion scenarios are detected and resolved deterministically.
2. Lock-order violations are prevented with explicit diagnostics.
3. Concurrency tests and targeted chaos scenarios validate robustness.
4. Logs include inheritance events and inversion-prevention outcomes.

## User Impact
High-priority interactions do not stall behind low-priority work.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:05.777889Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:27.508266Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","concurrency","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","performance","quality-gate","reactivity","reliability","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1196,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.2.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Execution split added for implementation rigor and testability:\n1. ft-2p9cb.2.3.1 (spec/contracts/benchmark/test matrix)\n2. ft-2p9cb.2.3.2 (deterministic implementation + guardrails)\n3. ft-2p9cb.2.3.3 (unit/property/integration/e2e + evidence/CI gates)\n\nDependency chain: ft-2p9cb.2.3.2 -> ft-2p9cb.2.3.1 and ft-2p9cb.2.3.3 -> ft-2p9cb.2.3.2.\nParent-child linkage keeps the subtask relationship explicit while preserving DAG integrity.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:27Z"}]}

@@ -3992,7 +3992,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.2","title":"[ARS] Extraction: MDL Sequencing & Symbolic Safety Proofs","description":"## Objective
Isolate the absolute minimal, mathematically safe command sequence required to transition the terminal back to a healthy state, generalizing it into a robust template.

## Subtasks
- **Minimum Description Length (MDL) Extraction:** Instead of simple edit distance, calculate the Kolmogorov complexity approximation of the recovery. Extract *only* the sequence of keystrokes that maximally compressed the error state back to the healthy state, discarding all intermediate agent flailing or failed retries.
- **Lightweight Symbolic Execution (Safety Guard):** Parse the extracted command AST. Run a fast symbolic execution pass to evaluate side effects. **Formally prove** the command does not traverse outside the captured CWD (rejecting `rm -rf ../*`) and does not leak captured entropy.
- **Secret & PII Redaction:** Run a highly optimized Aho-Corasick automaton over the sequence to unconditionally redact high-entropy secrets or PII.
- **Parameter Generalization & PAC Bounds:** Generalize parameters (e.g., file paths) using regex. Generate safety bounding regexes for the parameters and calculate the PAC-Bayesian bound on the risk of the generalization failing in similar contexts.

## Considerations & Future-Proofing
MDL extraction ensures we synthesize the *cure*, not the agent's *panic*. Symbolic Execution provides a formal guarantee that the machine won't write a virus, vastly increasing operator trust over simple string-matching blocklists.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. **MDL Verification:** Unit tests prove the MDL algorithm successfully isolates a single `npm install` fix out of a simulated trace containing 5 failed typo attempts, achieving minimal description length.
2. **Symbolic Safety:** The Symbolic Execution engine mathematically rejects path traversal (`../../`) and recursive destructive commands (`rm -rf /`), emitting a formal safety proof failure.
3. **Redaction:** Aho-Corasick scanner guarantees 100% redaction of injected mock AWS keys in < 1ms.
4. **Parameter Bounds:** Generalization correctly templates variables and attaches strict Regex bounds (e.g., `^[a-zA-Z0-9_./-]+$`).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:44:44.489386Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:10:23.086111Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.2","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.2","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.2.1","title":"[ARS][T2.1] Minimum Description Length (MDL) Command Extraction","description":"## Background & Justification
Agents flail. They type `ls`, typo commands, and try things that fail. 
We must extract only the *minimal* causal sequence of commands that successfully transitioned the state, discarding the noise.
We use Minimum Description Length (Kolmogorov complexity approximation) rather than simple edit distance.

## Considerations
The algorithm must scan the input buffer history between Infection and Recovery, utilizing `OSC 133` to identify discrete command blocks. It isolates the shortest logical pipeline that correlates with the `ProcessExit(0)`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Unit tests prove the algorithm extracts `npm install` and discards `npn intsall` and `ls -la` from a messy simulated trace.
2. Supports multi-command pipelines (e.g., extracting `rm -rf dist && build` as a single logical unit).
3. Evaluates in < 5ms for traces containing up to 100 intermediate commands.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.342730Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T21:02:16.118015Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T21:02:16.117960Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.2.1","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.342730Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.2.1","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.342730Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.2.2","title":"[ARS][T2.2] High-Speed Secret & PII Redaction via Aho-Corasick","description":"## Background & Justification
Extracted commands might inadvertently contain leaked secrets (AWS keys, passwords). These must never be cached or federated.

## Considerations
Implement an Aho-Corasick automaton loaded with hundreds of known high-entropy token patterns. It runs over the MDL-extracted command. If a secret is found, the ARS pipeline for this cluster is permanently aborted (we do not attempt to scrub and guess, we simply refuse to learn an unsafe reflex).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Aho-Corasick scanner evaluates in < 1ms.
2. Hard-aborts ARS synthesis if mock AWS keys, JWTs, or Slack tokens are detected in the command string.
3. -{"id":"ft-tkpgy.2.3","title":"[ARS][T2.3] Lightweight AST Symbolic Execution for Traversal/Destruction Safety","description":"## Background & Justification
A regex blocklist is not enough. To formally prove a reflex is safe, we parse its Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) using a fast shell lexer and symbolically execute it against a mock filesystem tree.

## Considerations
We must mathematically prove the command does not mutate paths outside the snapshotted CWD (e.g., rejecting `rm -rf ../*`) and does not invoke catastrophic binaries (`mkfs`, `dd`).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The AST lexer correctly parses standard `sh`/`bash` syntax.
2. Symbolic execution engine emits a formal safety proof failure if it detects directory traversal or recursive deletion targeting root.
3. Safely passes benign commands (e.g., `cargo build`, `npm install`).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.444971Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:10:28.302901Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.444971Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.444971Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.2.4","title":"[ARS][T2.4] Parameter Generalization and PAC-Bayesian Bounds Generation","description":"## Background & Justification
A hardcoded fix for `/app/src/main.rs` is useless if the error happens in `/app/src/lib.rs`. We must generalize parameters using regex capture groups.
Furthermore, we must generate strict Regex safety bounds for the variables (PAC-Bayesian bounded) to prevent injection vulnerabilities.

## Considerations
Compare the error text against the extracted command. If substrings match (e.g., filenames, line numbers), convert them to Jinja templates `{{cap.file}}`. Automatically attach a safety constraint like `^[a-zA-Z0-9_./-]+$`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Correctly identifies and templates dynamic variables shared between the MinHash cluster sample and the MDL command.
2. Auto-generates regex bounds that strictly reject path traversals, wildcards, or subshell injections (`$(...)`, `` `...` ``).
3. Provides trace logs documenting the exact generalization map and bounded constraints.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.496026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:10:27.925459Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.2.4","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.496026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.2.4","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.496026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.3","title":"[ARS] Compilation: FST Generation & Evidence Ledgers","description":"## Objective
Compile the safe, minimal template into an ultra-fast execution artifact and an immutable Evidence Ledger.

## Subtasks
- **Finite State Transducer (FST) Compilation:** Compile the MinHash signatures and trigger rules of all active reflexes into a Finite State Transducer. This guarantees that checking an incoming error against the entire ARS database takes $O(Length)$ time, completely independent of the number of reflexes stored.
- **Dynamic YAML Generation:** Construct the `WorkflowDescriptor` injecting `SnapshotTerminal` and `WaitFor` verification steps.
- **Dynamic Timeout via Expected Loss:** Calculate the dynamic timeout using an expected loss minimization function based on the tracked duration: $rg\min_t L(t) = 	ext{Cost(Hang)} \cdot P(T > t) + 	ext{Cost(PrematureKill)} \cdot P(T \le t)$.
- **Evidence Ledger Generation:** Append a cryptographic Evidence Ledger to the compiled reflex, detailing the exact Bayes Factors, MDL scores, and Symbolic Execution proofs that justified its synthesis.

## Considerations & Future-Proofing
FST compilation is an extreme software optimization that allows the immune system to scale to millions of known errors without ever slowing down the terminal processing loop.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. **Performance:** Benchmarks (`hyperfine` / `cargo bench`) prove the FST lookup evaluates against 100,000 mock reflex signatures in < 100µs.
2. **Compilation Integrity:** YAML serialization unit tests verify structural perfection, correct dynamic timeout calculations via the loss function, and snapshot/verification injections.
3. **Evidence Ledger:** The generated reflex contains a verifiable Evidence Ledger documenting the formal proofs of safety and minimal extraction.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:44:44.765616Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:10:22.725231Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.3","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.3","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.3.1","title":"[ARS][T3.1] Expected Loss Dynamic Timeout Calculator","description":"## Background & Justification
Reflexes must not hang. We use the wall-clock execution duration captured in T1.4 to calculate a dynamic timeout.
Using an expected loss minimization function: $rg\min_t L(t) = 	ext{Cost(Hang)} \cdot P(T > t) + 	ext{Cost(PrematureKill)} \cdot P(T \le t)$.

## Considerations
Allows the system to derive an optimal timeout (e.g., 2.5x the observed duration, clamped between 500ms and 60s) formally rather than using magic numbers.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Unit tests calculate the optimal timeout given a distribution of observed durations and specific cost weights.
2. Clamps correctly to minimum (500ms) and maximum (60s) bounds.
3. Timeout value is made available to the YAML generation step.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.548106Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:10:27.582133Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.1","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.548106Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.1","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.548106Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
